title: Flaky Turkish Nduja Flatbread
description: A spicy, crumbly flatbread topped with creamy nduja and fermented kimchi, baked to flaky perfection.
instructions: In a large bowl whisk flour, salt, sugar and yeast together.
Add warm water and 1 tablespoon olive oil and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes until smooth.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth and let rise for 20 minutes until doubled.
Preheat oven to 220°C and place a pizza stone or baking sheet inside.
Divide dough into 4 equal pieces, roll each into a thin round and place on a parchment paper sheet.
Brush dough with remaining olive oil and sprinkle evenly with smoked paprika and oregano.
Spread softened nduja over each flatbread, then top with chopped kimchi and parsley.
Bake for 18 minutes until edges are golden and dough is firm.
Remove from oven, let cool for 2 minutes, then slice and serve warm.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 3 days and reheat briefly before serving.
